title: PolyPour Object
description: Copper pour polygon belonging to a signal; filled (solid or hatched) copper area.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: hatched
properties.description: True if the pour is hatched (grid pattern); False if solid.

properties.name: isolate
properties.description: Isolation clearance (minimum distance to other signals), in internal units.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: layer
properties.description: Layer number (1-based index into the layer stack).

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: orphans
properties.description: True if orphaned (electrically disconnected) polygon regions are kept.

properties.name: rank
properties.description: Rank for polygon priority; lower-rank polygons are subtracted from higher-rank ones.

properties.name: spacing
properties.description: Spacing between hatch fill lines, in internal units (used when hatched).

properties.name: thermals
properties.description: True if thermal relief connections are used at pads.

properties.name: thermalWidth
properties.description: Width of thermal relief connections at pads, in internal units.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Polygon outline width, in internal units.
